episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Was Sophie Wessex Targeted by Epstein and New Questions about Eugenie's Charity
episodes.description:
Today’s Lownie Report Podcast includes an exclusive look at the paperback edition of ‘Entitled’, as well as a discussion about some of the latest news regarding how Sophie Wessex met Epstein back in 1996 and the reporting this week that the Charity Commission is taking a closer look at Princess Eugenie’s Anti-Slavery Collective,  which appears to have raised a lot more money that it has actually spent on the cause for which it was founded. 

I also look at the increasing concerns about the national security angle of the Epstein story which takes in efforts by both Russia and China to target the Yorks, who were the perfect useful idiots for the regimes. 

As I mention, there are worrying authoritarian tendencies emerging in the UK, which has grown more corrupt in recent years and which has an approach to secrecy that seems more akin to countries much less democratic than Britain. 

We discuss Sarah Ferguson’s numerous and opaque finances and what the paperback reveals about the unexplained crossover between her profit making ventures like her company Ginger and Moss and her charity drives like ‘Brew for the Crew’ and a £50k loan to her company from an unknown source. 

We also cover the extraordinary lengths that the diplomatic service went to to avoid Andrew’s visits and the safe full of complaints in the Foreign Office about his behaviour, and how diplomats simply gave up complaining when it became abundantly clear that the Queen’s protection meant Andrew was untouchable. 

Finally I look at the strange circumstances around Jeffrey Epstein’s death which I cover in ‘Entitled’ and what his brother Mark Epstein told me about his doubts surrounding his brother's ‘suicide’.

00:14:05 Tim Shipman's warning: the potential military dimension of the Epstein files
00:15:09 Andrew as a "useful idiot" for Russian intelligence

00:32:03 How does this story end? Accountability, transparency & the new reign

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Podcast: Fergie and P Diddy & Andrew's Cruelty Against His Dog
episodes.description:
The updated paperback of 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'  comes out 21st May and is currently half price on Amazon! 

Pre-order here - https://www.amazon.co.uk/Entitled-Mountbatten-Windsor-Fergusons-bestselling-devastating/dp/0008775494/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0

Join Andrew Lownie on Substack here - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

Today's The Lownie Report Podcast, recorded last week, takes a look at just some of the revelations in the updated paperback of ‘Entitled’, which comes out on the 21st May. Since the hardback came out last year dozens of former staff and associates have spoken to me and many of them on the record. 

What they told me not only backs up what I wrote originally, but adds far more detail about the repeated patterns of behaviour that characterise the way the York family operate and how they have been consistently protected by the Royal Family and the state. 

We cover fresh evidence about Sarah Ferguson’s continued contact with Jeffrey Epstein years after she claimed to have cut ties, her relationship with a third convicted sex offender in P Diddy, and serious questions about Princess Eugenie’s Anti-Slavery Collective, where staff salaries were running at almost double the amount spent on actual charitable programmes.

There is also new testimony about Andrew’s behaviour on taxpayer-funded overseas trips, a deeply troubling account of what he requested at a golf club opening in 2004, and an eyewitness account of him kicking his own dog at a Sandringham shooting weekend while, apart from one brave witness, those around him said nothing.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Podcast: Is Someone Still Running Epstein's Network?
episodes.description:
The updated paperback of 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'  comes out 21st May and is currently half price on Amazon! 

Pre-order here - https://www.amazon.co.uk/Entitled-Mountbatten-Windsor-Fergusons-bestselling-devastating/dp/0008775494/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0

Join Andrew Lownie on Substack here - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

In this month's subscriber Q&A, I discuss some of the latest developments around the Epstein saga. With my updated paperback of Entitled going to print on 21st May, ten thousand new words, and a new book in the works, there's plenty to get into. 

From Melania Trump's extraordinary public statement on Epstein victims to what it tells us about the dynamics inside the White House, the story clearly has a long way to go. 

On the royal front, we discussed the palace cover-up, possible injunctions, the letters to police protection officers, the culture of deference that silences newspaper editors and ties the hands of judges. 

We also discuss the York sisters, the question of whether their Ascot invitations signal a power struggle between Charles and William, and why the double standards around victims, trafficking survivors on one hand and well-connected women on the other, remain so troubling.

We round off with the bigger picture: how widespread is the rot across the royal family? Why does transparency matter so urgently, from a proper gifts register to royal finances to opening Buckingham Palace? 

And is someone out there filling the role Epstein vacated? These are uncomfortable questions but they're the ones we have to keep asking. Norman Baker and I are launching a cross-party campaign for royal openness and more on that very soon.

00:00:00 Welcome and book updates
00:00:34 Melania's Epstein statement and what is really behind it
00:01:49 Should royal finances be fully audited?
00:02:36 Palace cover-ups, injunctions and suppression of the Andrew story
00:05:07 Harry versus the Daily Mail and Britain's constraints on free speech
00:06:10 Is Charles complicit and why he is vulnerable
00:07:46 Will Prince Andrew ever face trial?
00:08:58 Could UK women come forward against Andrew as with Russell Brand?
00:10:40 The York sisters, Ascot and a power struggle between Charles and William
00:12:13 Class, double standards and how we talk about victims
00:14:25 Royal family members who get it right and those who don't
00:15:47 Did Trump know Melania was going to speak out?
00:17:46 How far does royal corruption go across the wider family?
00:19:24 The case for a royal gifts register and real transparency
00:22:10 The entitlement culture, freebies, favours and accountability
00:22:38 Will William be any more transparent than Charles?
00:24:09 Why the monarchy is in a more dangerous position than it realises
00:25:51 Is someone filling the role Epstein left vacant?

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Fergie's Luxury Hide and Seek and Gordon Brown's Explosive Epstein Revelations
episodes.description:
The updated paperback of 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'  comes out 21st May and is currently half price on Amazon! 

Pre-order here - https://www.amazon.co.uk/Entitled-Mountbatten-Windsor-Fergusons-bestselling-devastating/dp/0008775494/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0

Join Andrew Lownie on Substack here - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

A bumper edition of the Lownie Report Podcast this week, looking at Sarah Ferguson’s game of luxury hide and seek, Gordon Brown’s bombshell intervention in the Epstein affair, and a look at the myriad of ways that the establishment is still covering up for Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor.

 Sarah Ferguson remains one of the great mysteries of the moment. I helped to break the story of her stay in a top Swiss health retreat, and since then she has been playing  a game of luxury hide and seek, Austrian spa resorts, sightings in Donegal, rumours of a £1.3 million documentary deal. 

What is clear is that she still may pose a significant risk to the monarchy, who will be keen to ensure she can do no more damage than she already has. Whether the briefing about books and TV deals is a negotiating tactic remains to be seen. 

The biggest story of the week, though, is Gordon Brown’s second intervention in the Epstein affair, a long, forensic and explosive piece in the New Statesman that deserves far more attention than it has received. 

Brown is a man of considerable political and moral authority, and when someone of his standing puts his energy behind this, it is hugely encouraging. We cover his key revelations, including material on Andrew’s request for a Royal Air Fleet, Fergie and a multi-million pound Nigerian oil deal, RAF bases used by Epstein at Andrew’s request, whether he broke the Official Secrets Act, and what the refusal of the Mutual Legal Assistance Treaty request from the US to interview Andrew tells us about how far up this goes.

episodes.title: Norman Baker & Andrew Lownie on the Rise and Fall of the House of York
episodes.description:
Join Norman Baker on Substack here! https://substack.com/@normanbaker2

Pre-order the paperback of Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York here. https://www.amazon.co.uk/Entitled-Mountbatten-Windsor-Fergusons-bestselling-devastating/dp/0008775494/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0

With just one month to go before the highly anticipated release of the paperback of Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York, it was a pleasure to talk to  about his his forensic account of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Sarah Ferguson. 

Andrew started writing it in 2021, completing a trilogy on unusual royal marriages, but what began as a study of entitlement and arrogance has become something rather more serious. 

Andrew’s research makes clear that the problem is not a few bad apples but something endemic. The Government, the late Queen, Charles, the civil service and the diplomatic service all played their part in protecting a man who, as far as anyone can tell, feels no remorse whatsoever and continues to fret about where to park his cars.

The Epstein connection remains unresolved on both sides of the Atlantic, and Andrew had good reason to feel untouchable. The mutual legal assistance treaty request the Americans put forward was rejected by the British government. The DOJ now appears unwilling to share material with British police. Andrew has been destroying evidence. And the £12 million paid to Virginia Giuffre, drawn it seems from the Queen, Prince Philip’s estate and Charles, was hush money. People at the very top knew exactly what they were dealing with and chose to bury it.

Andrew also described his extraordinary battle with FOI requests, hundreds submitted over five years, almost nothing released, material locked away until 2065 in defiance of the 20 year rule. The Royal Archives has no public inventory. Files that were open have been withdrawn. The monarchy relies on trust and moral authority, and what Andrew’s research shows is that the institution has been protecting private interest at the expense of public duty for decades. With the arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, we are perhaps in the foothills of accountability, but I won’t be holding my breath.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Could Sarah Ferguson Be Arrested as a Conspirator?
episodes.description:
The updated paperback of 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'  comes out 21st May and is currently half price on Amazon! 

Pre-order here - https://www.amazon.co.uk/Entitled-Mountbatten-Windsor-Fergusons-bestselling-devastating/dp/0008775494/ref=tmm_pap_swatch_0

Join Andrew Lownie on Substack here - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

The news this week that the Crown Prosecution Service is now providing advice to police investigating Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Peter Mandelson suggests that these cases may be entering a more serious phase. It does not mean charges are imminent, but it does indicate that investigators are now working in closer consultation with prosecutors as they assess where the evidence may lead.

It was therefore timely, and a pleasure, to welcome retired policeman Mike Brewer to The Lownie Report podcast this week for a wide-ranging discussion about the police investigation into Andrew, Peter Mandelson and what might be going on behind the scenes.

Mike spent almost 30 years in law enforcement with Avon and Somerset Police, working across areas ranging from counter-terrorism to child abuse, and later led an important historic child abuse investigation in the Falklands.

In this fascinating interview, he brings that experience to bear on the practical realities of a major police inquiry, explaining what an arrest means, why it may have been considered necessary, and how investigators are likely to proceed from here.

We discussed why investigators may have wanted to move quickly to preserve evidence and retain control of the inquiry, with Mike noting that material people assume has been deleted can often still be recovered. He also made the point that it will still be very difficult to successfully prosecute Mountbatten-Windsor and the problems that would emerge if he called the King as a witness.

Mike explained how there will be both covert and overt investigations running in parallel, how police will be weighing up whether Sarah Ferguson is of more use as a witness or whether she may be investigated as a conspirator in Andrew’s crimes, and he also made the point that, despite what many have said about the complexity of prosecuting Misconduct in Public Office, it is actually quite a simple crime to prove.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Was Queen Compos Mentis in Final Years?
episodes.description:
In this episode of the Lownie Report we discuss another extraordinary week in the Andrew story, from the revelation that Priti Patel blocked a US request to interview him to growing signs that the wider scandal may at last be getting more serious attention on both sides of the Atlantic. I look at what may be happening behind the scenes, why the palace still appears determined to keep a lid on events, and whether Andrew remains both exposed and protected at the same time.

I also examine the changing position of Beatrice and Eugenie, rumours that Sarah Ferguson may yet tell her side of the story, and the wider cast of socialites, fixers and intermediaries surrounding Andrew and Epstein. The discussion broadens into the culture of secrecy around the royal family, the reluctance of politicians to speak plainly once they approach power, and the deeper question of whether meaningful scrutiny of royal conduct is ever really permitted.

Later in the episode, I consider the significance of claims about the late Queen’s condition in her final months, what that might mean for Charles’s responsibility in the Andrew affair, and whether the monarchy knowingly protected him for years. I also touch on Andrew’s continuing attachment to royal status, widening police interest, unresolved questions around deaths linked to the Epstein world, and renewed attention on Epstein’s New Mexico property, Zorro Ranch.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Why Did Establishment Cover-Up Last Decades
episodes.description:
It was great to have former Head of Royal Protection Dai Davies back on The Lownie Report podcast a couple of weeks ago, where he joined me to discuss the extraordinary developments following the arrest and questioning of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and Peter Mandelson.

With decades of experience inside the police, Dai offered a fascinating insider’s perspective on how investigations of this scale actually unfold. As he explained, early morning raids are often designed to catch suspects off guard and prevent evidence from disappearing.

He believes the police would have spent weeks gathering intelligence and coordinating with other agencies before seeking a warrant. The fact that officers then spent several days searching Royal Lodge suggests they were looking for a significant amount of material.

We also discussed the wider implications of the case and the difficult questions it raises about accountability when allegations involve members of the establishment. As Dai put it, the story has many tentacles, and the coming months will show whether investigators are genuinely prepared to follow them wherever they lead.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Podcast: Did Andrew Commit Treason?
episodes.description:
Support Andrew on Substack Here! https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

This episode of the Lownie Report Podcast takes a further look at what the intelligence report I revealed this week tells us about Andrew’s motivations, and the vulnerabilities that foreign secret services were able to take advantage of.

I also look at the intriguing news that there have been multiple resignations from Charles’s King’s Foundation, and why this might augur bad news in the coming weeks, and how it looks a little like people abandoning a sinking ship.

Are there echoes of the Pitch@Palace fiasco? Did the funding of Dumfries House cross ethical boundaries? I discuss how a friend of mine who served in MI6 visited the Scottish mansion recently and was surprised to see so many names in the donors’ book that raised alarm bells.

The episode also looks at the Yorks’ wider history with charities. We discuss Eugenie’s anti-slavery charity, the amounts raised, what the money is spent on, and the ways in which Sarah Ferguson and Andrew in the past have spent charity money in questionable ways. Indeed, Andrew had to pay back huge sums that he had paid to Amanda Thirsk due to charity rules that had been breached by such payments.

We cover the critical question of the revolving door between the government, monarchy, military and the intelligence services, and how this has helped to protect the Royal Family and allowed them to entirely avoid scrutiny.
Finally, we look at the long and murky history of royal servants, money-making, and how royal gifts are still ambiguous, despite the supposed checks and balances meant to be provided by the Royal Gifts Register.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Podcast: What's Going on with Beatrice and Eugenie?
episodes.description:
In this episode of The Lownie Report, we take a deep dive into Sarah Ferguson and the long history of scandal, entitlement and money-making that has followed her for decades. We begin though with the growing scrutiny around Princess Beatrice and Princess Eugenie, the changing media mood, and the sense that the Royal Family may be trying to distance itself before further damaging revelations emerge.

We then go back to the beginning of the Fergie story: her family background, her marriage to Prince Andrew, the speed of their decline in public popularity, and the way in which royal status was increasingly used for personal advantage. Andrew explains how Ferguson changed after joining the Royal Family, why Prince Philip came to view her as a liability, and how the marriage quickly became more of an arrangement than a romance.

The discussion also examines Ferguson’s increasingly questionable circle, her relationship with Jeffrey Epstein, her habit of rewriting the narrative around her own scandals, and the dilemma she now poses for the monarchy. If she feels she has nothing left to lose, could she yet become a serious threat to the institution she spent years exploiting?

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Andrew's Arrest, What the King Knew and the Russia Connection.
episodes.description:
Join Andrew on Substack here! - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

In this explosive episode of The Lownie Report, Andrew Lownie and James Fairclough examine the dramatic arrest of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor and the growing scrutiny surrounding Peter Mandelson. Was the King warned years ago about Andrew’s business dealings? Did the Palace ignore intelligence briefings? And is this now becoming a full-scale national security crisis?

From Sarah Ferguson’s company closures to allegations of document destruction, from whistleblowers coming forward to an alleged Russian intelligence dossier, this episode explores whether we are witnessing the beginning of the most serious reckoning the modern monarchy has ever faced. Is this the tipping point?

episodes.title: Andrew Arrested! The Lownie Report Emergency Podcast
episodes.description:
A historic day as Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor is arrested on his 66th birthday, accused of Misconduct in Public Office. I have been calling for this for over 6 months since ‘Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York’ was published and I am hugely gratified that the police are finally taking Andrew’s alleged crimes seriously.

The Lownie Report is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

It’s also highly significant that police have called for victims to come forward in regards to Andrew’s alleged sexual crimes and that 8 police forces are now assessing claims across all of Mountbatten-Windsor’s alleged crimes is both encouraging and a sign of the breadth of evidence there is against him.

Questions will have to asked of what on earth prevented the police from taking action until now and whether there has been a sustained Royal cover up and staff such as Dominic Hampshire and Amanda Thirsk, and his accountant Arthur Lancaster, will need to be questioned thoroughly as to their role, if any, in Andrew’s alleged crimes.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Monarchy in Crisis and Will Andrew Go to Jail
episodes.description:
Support Andrew on Substack Here! - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie

This week on The Lownie Report, we examine the accelerating fallout from the latest Epstein-related disclosures and their growing political and constitutional impact. As media scrutiny intensifies and public pressure mounts, questions of accountability within the Royal Household — and beyond — are no longer confined to the margins but are entering the mainstream conversation.

Andrew Lownie discusses the responses from the King, the Prince and Princess of Wales, and the broader institutional handling of the crisis, alongside emerging issues involving intelligence briefings, record-keeping, press scrutiny, and potential police developments. The conversation also explores the widening circle of figures touched by the scandal and the implications for the monarchy’s credibility and public trust.

From the role of royal staff and international reporting disparities to publishing, reputation management, and future investigations, this episode offers context, analysis, and perspective on one of the most consequential royal controversies in decades.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: The Walls are Closing in On Andrew
episodes.description:
A major new release of Epstein Files material has intensified scrutiny of Andrew and Peter Mandelson's relationship with Jeffrey Epstein and raised broader questions about accountability, transparency, and elite power in Britain.

In this episode of The Lownie Report, Andrew Lownie joins James Fairclough to examine the latest disclosures and their implications. They discuss allegations involving Andrew and Sarah Ferguson, questions surrounding Peter Mandelson, and the possibility of legal or parliamentary investigations. The conversation also explores the royal family’s response, Freedom of Information exemptions, intelligence concerns, and the wider political context.

From trade envoy activities and international business links to the symbolic management of royal crises, this discussion situates current developments within a long historical pattern of establishment protection and public pressure.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report Emergency Podcast: Why Latest Epstein Release is Most Devastating Yet
episodes.description:
Join Andrew on Substack here! - https://andrewlownie.substack.com/p/epstein-files-are-proof-that-the?r=39c5sl

An extra episode of the podcast this week due to the astonishing revelations that have emerged in the last 48 hours. Andrew Lownie and James Fairclough look at what the implications are for all of the principal characters and why this might be a tipping point where the police feel they have no choice but to take action.

We spoke about why King Charles needs to ensure he doesn’t make the same mistakes as his mother, about the KGB/FSB, Putin and Epstein link and about what we now know about the financial relationship between Epstein and Peter Mandelson. We also looked at the Sir Richard Branson connection and the PR advised he was giving to Epstein. 

We also discussed the China role in all of this and the news that Andrew met President Xi on many occasions, with birthday letters to Xi drafted by alleged Chinese spy Yang Tengbo. 

There is a huge amount to process so we will be covering all of this in much more detail in next week’s episode.

episodes.title: Michael Wolff on Why Epstein's Friends Didn't Care about His Conviction
episodes.description:
Why did so many powerful people continue to stand by Jeffrey Epstein even after his arrest and conviction? In this conversation, Michael Wolff explores the social, cultural and political forces that allowed Epstein to remain protected, welcomed, and taken seriously long after the warning signs were clear.

Wolff discusses Epstein’s deep entanglement with Donald Trump, the modelling world of the 1990s, and the unanswered questions surrounding Melania Trump, explaining how status, money and mutual interests created a closed circle resistant to scrutiny.

The discussion also examines why Epstein was feared rather than exposed, why damaging material was never released, and why accountability consistently failed — revealing a deeper contradiction at the heart of elite power and American public life.

00:13:40 Epstein and the British royal circle
00:18:15 Deutsche Bank, money and leverage
00:21:15 Rewriting history after the fact
00:24:40 Why no one wants to publish an Epstein book

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Andrew and Fergie's PR Fightback
episodes.description:
This week on The Lownie Report, Andrew looks at the increasingly confused and contradictory messaging coming out of the House of York.

Andrew’s apparent estrangement from Princess Eugenie is followed almost immediately by a carefully staged photo opportunity with Princess Beatrice and her daughter in Windsor Great Park — raising fresh questions about loyalty, money, and PR strategy.

We also examine the Yorks’ continued financial links to the Middle East, Sarah Ferguson’s unresolved role in the Epstein network, tensions within the wider royal family, and what all this tells us about power, money, and survival inside the modern monarchy.

episodes.title: Michael Wolff: Epstein Was Afraid of Trump
episodes.description:
It was an honour and a privilege to interview Michael Wolff about his extraordinary career that has recently seen him as the key chronicler of Donald Trump, Rupert Murdoch and Jeffrey Epstein. 

We spoke about how Jeffrey Epstein met Princess Diana more than once, about how Epstein was afraid of Trump and about Epstein's meeting with Putin, and how it may have been Michael's book Siege that ultimately led to Epstein's arrest. We also discussed the truth behind the Melania and Epstein connection. 

We also discussed why Epstein's friends looked past his conviction for procuring a minor and why he was so intent on trying to bring down President Trump and why he was ultimately unsuccessful. 

Michael has bravely taken on Melania Trump in a lawsuit that sees him taking action where many far more powerful news organisations seem content to not upset the President and his wife. It's no surprise that thousands of people have donated to his gofundme and I think it's vital that anyone with a few spare pounds considers helping him in his David vs Goliath battle.

https://www.gofundme.com/f/help-michael-wolff-in-his-legal-fight-against-melania-trump

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: Eugenie's Split with Andrew and What’s Really Going on Behind the Scenes?
episodes.description:
Join Andrew on Substack here! - https://substack.com/@andrewlownie?utm_source=user-menu

In this episode of The Lownie Report, historian and royal biographer Andrew Lownie joins James Fairclough to examine the accelerating crisis surrounding Andrew, from his rapid exit from Royal Lodge to reports that Princess Eugenie has cut off contact with her father.

The discussion explores what may be happening behind the scenes: coordinated PR briefings, the role of Sarah Ferguson, the Palace’s crisis management strategy, and whether new revelations connected to Jeffrey Epstein are imminent.

Lownie also revisits the Newsnight interview, the BBC’s handling of the Andrew story, intelligence and law-enforcement reluctance to act, and the long-term risks facing Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie as they try to protect their futures.

episodes.title: The Lownie Report: The Epstein Questions Andrew Needs to Answer
episodes.description:
In this latest episode of The Lownie Report, Andrew Lownie discusses the latest Epstein disclosures — and what they reveal about Andrew, Sarah Ferguson, and the wider failure of institutions to pursue accountability.

Drawing on new material emerging from US court filings, Andrew examines Andrew’s continued silence in the face of repeated opportunities to assist law enforcement, the troubling financial networks that developed during and after his time as Trade Envoy, and the extent to which royal status appears to have shielded him from scrutiny.

We also discuss the implications for the monarchy itself, the role of the Metropolitan Police and the FBI, and why the drip-feed of revelations may yet prove far more damaging than a single moment of reckoning.

episodes.title: Andrew Lownie on Andrew and Fergie's Marriage, Fiery Rows and Arrested Development
episodes.description:
Andrew Lownie reveals the extraordinary inner world of Sarah Ferguson and Prince Andrew — entitlement, chaos, spending, scandal, staff mistreatment, arrested development, and decades of behaviour that shaped the downfall of the Yorks. Including how Andrew cleared the Royal Box at the Royal Albert Hall for him and his mistress. 

From their volatile marriage to selling access, financial recklessness, and their strange 30-year post-divorce partnership. 

Andrew Lownie’s bestselling and explosive new book 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York' is out now.

00:48:00 Parenting reality vs. public image
00:50:00 Sunninghill atmosphere, exclusion, and silent family distances
00:52:00 Gate-racing temper, smashed plates, Windsor anger patterns

00:56:00 Diana & Fergie: rivalry, leaks, reconciliation, fracture
00:58:00 Reputation management after 1997
01:00:00 Fergie & Epstein: logs, contact timeline, contradictions
01:02:00 The 2011 letter vs. later condemnations
01:04:00 Media choreography around interviews and PR containment
01:06:00 What Andrew and Fergie do now: private deals

01:16:00 Pattern of institutional containment until scandal breaks
01:18:00 What a real reckoning would require and what could move the needle next

episodes.title: Andrew Lownie on how the Palace Cover-up Prolonged the Abuse
episodes.description:
Andrew Lownie discusses the palace cover-up, how Andrew met Epstein and what he's found out since the release of his revelatory book 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'.

00:00:00 – Reaction to Andrew Lownie’s book & new stories since publication
00:01:04 – Senior insiders now willing to go on the record
00:02:03 – Calls for inquiry into Andrew’s finances and role as Special Representative
00:02:49 – Could more extreme Epstein material still be released?

00:03:56 – Epstein, Andrew & Kazakhstan tales
00:04:12 – How Andrew & Epstein first met – Late 1990s or earlier?
00:05:23 – Fergie’s introduction & the disputed timeline
00:06:17 – Sarah Ferguson’s friendship with Epstein 
00:07:30 – Girlfriends of Andrew linked to Epstein and Trump
00:08:17 – Scale of witness testimony beyond Virginia Giuffre
00:09:52 – BBC Panorama, Amy Robach & the palace blocking media stories
00:12:18 – Did the palace prolong abuse by covering up Epstein links?
00:13:26 – Was Andrew blackmailed? Epstein, kompromat & intelligence services
00:14:43 – Palace complaints ignored; Queen’s blind spot for Andrew
00:17:07 – Andrew’s close ties with Donald Trump

00:19:57 – Jean-Luc Brunel, trafficking, and French police interest in Andrew
00:21:26 – The real story behind Panorama vs Newsnight
00:23:41 – Laura Burns’ role erased from history
00:24:17 – The Robert Maxwell connection? 
00:26:27 – Epstein’s state backing, Saudi passport & Mossad links
00:28:32 – Was Andrew naive or a willing traitor?
00:31:18 – John Mark Dougan & the missing Epstein tapes in Russia
00:33:10 – Financial ties between Andrew & Ghislaine Maxwell
00:34:08 – Trump as supposed FBI informant? Andrew Lownie’s verdict
00:35:36 – Epstein’s “scientific” respectability & Andrew’s supposed curiosity
00:38:21 – What surprised Andrew Lownie most in his research
00:39:20 – Epstein’s “photo books” & Andrew ordering girls from a catalogue
00:40:39 – Why did the Met Police refuse to investigate Andrew?
00:42:29 – Charles lending money for Andrew’s Giuffre settlement
00:44:11 – Charles’ Balmoral “crisis summit” and PR damage control
00:46:14 – William’s likely ruthlessness vs Andrew in future
00:46:53 – Parallels with Mountbatten scandal cover-up
00:48:45 – Fifth Amendment: why so many refused to answer about Andrew
00:50:54 – NDAs, bugging staff, and Andrew’s inappropriate behaviour

episodes.title: Andrew Lownie on Prince Andrew and the Queen's Moral Abdication
episodes.description:
I talked to Andrew Lownie about 'Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York'. We covered Prince Andrew and Epstein, how the Queen knew there were concerns about his behaviour, and the stuff that he couldn't put in the book.

00:00:00 – Jacob Rees-Mogg interview aftermath 
00:02:00 – Royal scandals historically suppressed
00:05:00 – Lownie’s path from intelligence to royal books
00:10:00 – NDAs, spies & nervous sources in Kazakhstan

00:15:00 – Fergie’s debts, Epstein’s role & the divine right of kings
00:19:00 – Rage and bullying: Windsor family tradition
00:20:00 – Queen refused to protect Andrew early, then compensated victims
00:22:00 – The Queen knew exactly what was going on
00:25:00 – MI5 “dry-cleaned” Fergie’s past
00:27:00 – Moral abdication: why the Queen shielded Andrew
00:29:00 – Institutional dishonesty in the monarchy
00:30:00 – Andrew admits: “We’ve been lying to the press for 20 years”
00:31:00 – Charles’s scandals and Middle Eastern money
00:34:00 – Offshore trusts, Sunninghill and suspicious deals

00:41:42 - The Peter Mandelson Connection
